description: §49.4.4 — `let x = while (...) { ... lift ... }` puts a `while` in an expression position WITHOUT the §49.6 `lift`-accumulator pattern (the body lifts, but the value is bound by direct assignment rather than consumed via `~`), firing E-LOOP-007. TRIGGER NOTE (ss75, 2026-07-16): the live fire condition is NARROWER than the code's own registry rows describe — type-system.ts:18672 requires the while body to CONTAIN `lift` (regex `/^while\s*\(...\)\s*\{...\blift\b...\}$/`) plus an `escape-hatch` initExpr. Its source comment states the narrowing is a deliberate workaround for an ast-builder misparse that absorbs an unrelated while-stmt into a preceding let-decl's init. Consequence: the SPEC's OWN §49.4.4 example `let x = while (...) { ... }` (no lift) does NOT fire — see the under-fire hole escalated with this case. W-PROGRAM-001 co-fires incidentally (no `<program>` shell); not asserted.
rationale: §49.4.4: 'A bare `while` statement SHALL NOT be used as an expression. The compiler SHALL reject any attempt to use a `while` statement in an expression position (e.g., `let x = while (...) { ... }`) without the `lift` accumulator pattern (§49.6). The error is E-LOOP-007.' §49.4.4 further fixes the sole exception: 'A `while` statement in a `lift`-accumulating context (§49.6) is valid as an expression position assignment target via the `~` accumulator. This is the only exception.' §49.6.1 fixes that pattern's shape: the `while` stays a STATEMENT and the accumulated values are consumed by a following `let result = ~`. This case uses `lift` but binds via direct assignment, not via `~`, so it is NOT the §49.6 pattern and NOT the sole exception — it is an expression-position `while` and SHALL be rejected. §34 and §49.9 both register E-LOOP-007 at severity Error. WORDING TENSION (escalated, not decided here): the one-line rows at §34 and §49.9 compress the condition to '`while` used as an expression without `lift`/`~`', which read literally would NOT fire on this case (its body does contain `lift`). The normative prose of §49.4.4 — which conditions on the full §49.6 `lift`+`~` accumulator PATTERN, not on the bare presence of a `lift` token — governs and does fire.
